fault_hypothesis: Line 66 uses `int(x)` (truncation) instead of a rounding strategy that conserves the total, causing the sum of allocations to be less than `total` when fractional remainders are truncated away.

constraints.intent: After building integer allocations via int(), their sum must equal the total; truncation causes this to fail.
The integer allocations should sum to the rounded total of planned shares, not be truncated below it.
Before returning, the final allocations must sum to within 1 unit of the total; truncation violates this for non-divisible inputs.
At the end of the integer-allocation branch, the sum of allocated units must equal the total input.
The returned allocations must sum to within 1 unit of the requested total.
